channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Review: Samsung latest watch and smart ring
item.description: When it comes to health and fitness tracking, Samsung has you covered. The company has everything from the ultra-affordable to the ultra-rugged - plus a ring that can track your stats 24/7. Their lineup starts with the $200 Galaxy Watch FE, which gives you the basics at an affordable price.
item.dc:creator: Rich DeMuro, KTLA 5

item.title: Plane makes emergency landing in New York City after pilot dies mid-flight
item.description: Crew members on the Turkish Airlines flight worked to revive the pilot, but he died before the plane landed.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Israeli offensive in hard-hit northern Gaza kills dozens and threatens hospitals
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — A large-scale Israeli operation in northern Gaza has killed dozens of people and threatens to shut down three hospitals over a year into the war with Hamas, Palestinian officials and residents said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton as it churns toward Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: A powerful Hurricane Milton churned through the Gulf of Mexico toward Florida on Wednesday and could wallop one of the state's major population centers with huge storm surges, lashing rain and destructive winds just two weeks after deadly Hurricane Helene swamped the coastline.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Time to evacuate is running out as Hurricane Milton closes in on Florida
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — A steady rain fell in the Tampa Bay area Wednesday morning as a mighty Hurricane Milton churned toward a potentially catastrophic collision with the west coast of Florida, where some residents insisted they would stay even after millions were ordered to evacuate.
item.dc:creator: TERRY SPENCER and HAVEN DALEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Alfonso Cuarón and Cate Blanchett made a television series. 'Disclaimer' is already a triumph
item.description: VENICE, Italy (AP) — “Disclaimer” pulls the rug out from the audience before they’ve had the chance to get settled. There’s no building of empathy for its central character, Cate Blanchett’s Catherine Ravenscroft.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Over 9 million pounds of ready-to-eat meat, poultry recalled: USDA
item.description: The products may be contaminated with Listeria monocytogenes, according to the U.S. Department of Agriculture.
item.dc:creator: Michael Reiner, KRON4

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: A retired Houston officer gets 60 years in a couple's drug raid deaths that revealed corruption
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — A former Houston police officer was sentenced to 60 years in prison on Tuesday for the murder of a married couple during a drug raid that revealed systemic corruption in the department’s narcotics unit and prompted criticism of the law enforcement agency and its tactics.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Plane evacuating people from Hurricane Milton crashes after takeoff
item.description: A plane carrying passengers evacuating from Hurricane Milton crashed near the southeast corner of the St. Pete Pier Tuesday morning, according to police.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Candidates for Las Vegas Mayor face off in a debate ahead of Election Day
item.description: Shelley Berkley will face off against Victoria Seaman in the non-partisan race. That debate will also be hosted by Denise Valdez, John Langeler, and Joshua Peguero.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, NewsNation

item.title: Michigan US Senate debate: Rogers, Slotkin race could determine control
item.description: Rogers and Slotkin meet on Tuesday for an hour-long debate hosted by Nexstar's WOOD-TV, beginning at 7 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Van Gilder, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What makes a storm a hurricane? The dangers across 5 categories
item.description: Severe weather can target any part of the globe, but each year the Atlantic hurricane season brings heavy rain, strong wind, storm surge and sometimes catastrophic devastation to locations from the Caribbean islands and Central America all the way up through the United States and into Canada.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Cruz opponent Allred raises $30 million in 3 months
item.description: Rep. Colin Allred (D-Texas) announced Tuesday that he has raised more than $30 million over the past three months in his battle against incumbent Texas Sen. Ted Cruz (R), reflecting broad desire among Democratic donors to defeat one of the Senate’s most prominent conservatives.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Is it safe to be outside in Hurricane Milton's eye? Here's what experts say
item.description: The eye of a hurricane is usually calm compared to the storm swirling around it — but is it safe to be outside as the eye passes over you?
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Airline apologizes after playing explicit movie on every screen
item.description: "Daddio," the movie ultimately selected to play during the 10-hour flight, according to reports, features Dakota Johnson and Sean Penn and has an "R" rating.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: 20 lbs. of cocaine found in truck floorboard at South Texas port of entry
item.description: A truck driven by a U.S. citizen had over 20 pounds of cocaine hidden in the floorboards as it tried to enter the South Texas border from Mexico, U.S. Customs and Border Protection officials said.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: Floridians prepare for the worst as Hurricane Milton quickly approaches
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor took to television with a dire warning for those staying behind in evacuation zones ahead of Hurricane Milton's landfall.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: States sue TikTok, alleging platform is addictive, harms kids' mental health
item.description: More than a dozen states and the District of Columbia sued TikTok on Tuesday, alleging the platform exploits and harms young users while "deceiving" the public about these dangers.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, ABC 4

item.title: Is it safe to be outside in Hurricane Milton's eye? Here's what experts say
item.description: The eye of a hurricane is usually calm compared to the storm swirling around it — but is it safe to be outside as the eye passes over you?
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, ABC 4

item.title: U.S. Postal Service announces recommended 2024 holiday shipping dates
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service announced its recommended mailing and shipping dates on Tuesday for the 2024 holiday season. The following list shows the latest recommended dates for a package sent to an address in the contiguous United States to be delivered by Dec.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KTLA 5

item.title: Deadspin loses bid to toss defamation suit over article accusing young Chiefs fan of racism
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — A Delaware judge has refused to dismiss a defamation lawsuit against sports website Deadspin over an article accusing a 9-year-old NFL fan and his family of racism because of his game-day attire. The lawsuit was filed by California residents Raul Armenta Jr.
item.dc:creator: RANDALL CHASE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Melania Trump waves off 'hand-swatting incident': A 'mere misunderstanding'
item.description: Melania Trump is waving off speculation that she didn't want to hold her husband's hand, chalking up images of her seemingly "swatting" away former President Donald Trump to a "mere misunderstanding.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Fearful residents flee Tampa Bay region as Hurricane Milton takes aim at Florida coast
item.description: Fearful Florida residents streamed out of the Tampa Bay region Tuesday ahead of what could be a once-in-a-century direct hit from Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: DeSantis pushes back on 'delusional' Harris remark about Hurricane Milton calls
item.description: Florida Gov. Ron DeSantis (R) said Monday that it was “delusional” for Vice President Kamala Harris to accuse him of “political gamesmanship” after reports emerged that he did not take the Democratic presidential nominee’s calls as his state braces for Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump campaign blasts Bob Woodward; denies secret Putin calls
item.description: Donald Trump's campaign is rejecting claims in Bob Woodward's new book 'War' about the former president holding secret calls with Vladimir Putin since leaving office.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Canyoneer dies after accidental 150-foot fall at Zion National Park
item.description: A man fell while canyoneering at Zion National Park on Saturday, resulting in his death, according to the National Park Service.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Bittan, WGN 9

item.title: Why is Tampa Bay so vulnerable to hurricanes?
item.description: As Milton barrels toward the Gulf Coast of Florida, experts are worried that a century of luck could come to a sudden end.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Harris reveals owning a Glock: 'Of course' I've fired it
item.description: Vice President Kamala Harris said in a "60 Minutes" interview that aired Monday that she owns a Glock and has fired it at a shooting range in another example of how she and her campaign have made a point to highlight her gun ownership.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: Purple Whoppers are coming to Burger King for a limited time
item.description: Burger King is teaming up with Amazon MGM Studios to release a new menu inspired by The Addams Family. The Addams Family Menu includes four items that will be inspired by an Addams family member, according to the burger chain.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: What's fueling Costco's gold rush?
item.description: Move over, gas and hot dogs. Costco shoppers are snatching up the store's 1-ounce gold bars despite the market's lofty price for the precious metal.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KTLA 5

item.title: Singer Cissy Houston, mother of Whitney Houston, dies at 91
item.description: Cissy Houston, the mother of the late Whitney Houston and a two-time Grammy winner who performed alongside superstar musicians like Elvis Presley, and Aretha Franklin, has died. She was 91.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: You may be eligible for lawsuit payout if you ate Breyers vanilla ice cream
item.description: A court ruling on the settlement has not yet occurred, with a final approval hearing set for Nov. 21.
item.dc:creator: Alonzo Small, Fox 5

item.title: Al Pacino claims he almost died of COVID: 'I was gone'
item.description: During a podcast appearance on The New York Times’ “The Interview," Al Pacino claims he once nearly died of COVID.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: James Taylor, Luke Combs, Eric Church to take part in Helene relief concert in NC
item.description: "Concert for Carolina," featuring North Carolina natives James Taylor, Luke Combs and Eric Church, will be held at Bank of America Stadium in Charlotte with all proceeds going to Hurricane Helene relief efforts across the Carolina region, organizers announced.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Ullmann, KRON4

item.title: Judge rules the FTC can proceed with antitrust lawsuit against Amazon, tosses out few state claims
item.description: A federal judge said the Federal Trade Commission can proceed with its landmark antitrust lawsuit against Amazon. But, he also gave the company a small victory by tossing out a few claims made by states involved in the legal fight. The order, issued last week by Judge John H.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Colleges brace for Oct. 7 anniversary
item.description: Colleges face a major test Monday as pro-Palestinian and pro-Israel students both hold events for the first anniversary of Hamas’s attack on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, ABC 4
